facts.answer: Nice drawing! Want me to search for more info about it?
JavaScript is a scripting language that runs in browsers and Node.js. It handles interactivity, logic, and DOM manipulation. It's the language of the web.
TypeScript is JavaScript with types. It catches bugs at compile time. You write .ts files and compile them to .js.

HTML (HyperText Markup Language) structures web pages. Elements like
,

, , define the content.
CSS (Cascading Style Sheets) styles HTML — colors, fonts, layout, animations. Use flexbox or grid for layouts.
Python is great for scripting, data science, AI, and web backends. It's known for clean, readable syntax. pip is its package manager.
Java is a strongly-typed, compiled language. It runs on the JVM (Java Virtual Machine), making it cross-platform. Common in enterprise and Android apps.
Kotlin is the modern language for Android development. It runs on the JVM and is fully interoperable with Java, but much more concise.
Swift is Apple's language for iOS and macOS apps. It replaced Objective-C and is much more modern and safe.
Rust is a systems programming language focused on memory safety without a garbage collector. It's fast and prevents many classes of bugs.
Go (Golang) is Google's language — fast, simple, great for backend services and CLI tools. Known for goroutines (lightweight concurrency).
C++ is a powerful low-level language used in games, operating systems, and high-performance software. It's complex but very fast.
PHP is a server-side scripting language mostly used for web development. WordPress runs on PHP.
Ruby is an elegant scripting language known for Ruby on Rails, a popular web framework that made web development much faster.
SQL (Structured Query Language) queries relational databases. Key commands: SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, JOIN.
NoSQL databases like MongoDB store data as documents instead of tables. Good for flexible schemas and large scale.
React is a JavaScript UI library made by Meta. You build UIs with components. Use useState and useEffect for state and side effects.
Vue.js is a progressive JavaScript framework for building UIs. Simpler learning curve than React for many beginners.
Angular is Google's full-featured frontend framework. Uses TypeScript and is common in large enterprise apps.
Node.js lets you run JavaScript on the server. npm is its package manager. Great for APIs and real-time apps.
An API (Application Programming Interface) lets software talk to other software. REST APIs use HTTP. GraphQL is another approach.
REST APIs use HTTP methods: GET (read), POST (create), PUT/PATCH (update), DELETE (remove). Data usually comes back as JSON.
JSON (JavaScript Object Notation) is a lightweight data format. Keys and string values use double quotes. Great for APIs and config files.
Docker packages apps into containers — isolated environments that run the same everywhere. Use docker-compose for multi-container apps.
Kubernetes orchestrates Docker containers at scale. It handles deployment, scaling, and healing of containerized apps.
Git is a version control system. Key commands: git init, add, commit, push, pull, branch, merge, rebase, stash.
GitHub hosts Git repositories. You can collaborate, open pull requests, use Actions for CI/CD, and host static sites with GitHub Pages.
Linux is an open-source OS kernel. Most servers run Linux. Popular distros: Ubuntu, Debian, Fedora, Arch, Alpine.
Ubuntu is a popular Linux distro based on Debian. Package manager: apt. Good for servers and desktop use.
Termux is a terminal emulator for Android. It gives you a Linux environment on your phone/tablet. Package manager: pkg.
Bash is the default shell on most Linux systems. You write scripts with .sh extension. It handles file operations, loops, conditions, and pipelines.
Vim is a powerful terminal text editor. i to insert, Esc to exit insert mode, :w to save, :q to quit, :wq to save and quit.
localhost (127.0.0.1) is your own machine. When you run a dev server, you usually visit localhost:PORT in the browser.
HTTP is the protocol for web communication. HTTPS is the encrypted version using TLS/SSL. Always use HTTPS in production.
SSL/TLS encrypts web traffic. TLS is the modern version of SSL. Certificates are issued by Certificate Authorities (CAs). Let's Encrypt gives free certs.
DNS (Domain Name System) translates domain names to IP addresses. It's like the phone book of the internet.
An IP address identifies a device on a network. IPv4 looks like 192.168.1.1. IPv6 is longer and handles more addresses.
A VPN (Virtual Private Network) encrypts your internet traffic and hides your IP. Good for privacy and accessing geo-blocked content.
Firebase is Google's backend-as-a-service. It offers a real-time database, authentication, hosting, and cloud functions.
Supabase is an open-source Firebase alternative built on PostgreSQL. Great for auth, real-time data, and storage.
GitHub Pages hosts static websites directly from a GitHub repo for free. Perfect for HTML/CSS/JS projects.
Netlify is a hosting platform for static sites and JAMstack apps. It auto-deploys from GitHub and has a free tier.
Vercel is a hosting platform optimized for Next.js and frontend apps. Free tier available, deploys from GitHub.
npm is Node's package manager. npm install adds packages. package.json lists dependencies. Use npm run to run scripts.
Bundlers like Webpack and Vite package your JS, CSS, and assets for production. Vite is faster and simpler for modern projects.
Android is Google's mobile OS. Apps are built in Kotlin or Java. The Play Store distributes apps. APKs are the app package format.
iOS is Apple's mobile OS for iPhones and iPads. Apps are built in Swift or Objective-C and distributed via the App Store.
The Daylight DC-1 tablet runs a custom version of Android and uses a LivePaper display — a reflective, high-refresh-rate screen that's easy on the eyes.
Boox makes e-ink Android tablets. They're great for reading and writing with minimal eye strain. Run a full Android environment.
GitHub Actions automates workflows — CI/CD, testing, deployment — directly from your repo using YAML config files in .github/workflows/.
Markdown is a lightweight formatting language. # for headings, **bold**, *italic*, - for lists, ``` for code. GitHub renders it automatically.
Regex matches patterns in text. Common: \d (digit), \w (word char), .* (anything), ^ (start), $ (end). Test at regex101.com.
localStorage stores key-value data in the browser persistently. Use localStorage.setItem(key, value) and getItem(key). Limit ~5MB per origin.
sessionStorage is like localStorage but clears when the browser tab closes. Useful for temporary session data.
Cookies store small data in the browser. They can have expiry dates and are sent with every HTTP request to the server, unlike localStorage.
CORS (Cross-Origin Resource Sharing) controls which domains can access an API. Blocked CORS errors are common when a frontend calls a different backend domain.
WebSockets provide full-duplex real-time communication between browser and server. Great for chat apps, live updates, and games.
MCP (Model Context Protocol) is Anthropic's standard for connecting AI models to external tools and data sources. It lets AI assistants use apps like Google Drive, Slack, etc.
Claude is an AI assistant made by Anthropic. I'm Monkey Joe — a simpler rules-based assistant made by Akiva with Claude's help! 🐒
ChatGPT is OpenAI's AI chatbot powered by GPT models. It's one of the most widely used AI assistants. Claude (my maker) is made by Anthropic.
LLMs (Large Language Models) are AI systems trained on huge text datasets. They generate human-like text. Examples: Claude, GPT-4, Gemini, Llama.
Artificial Intelligence is machines simulating human thinking. Modern AI uses neural networks and machine learning. LLMs like Claude are a type of AI.
Machine learning trains models on data to make predictions or decisions without being explicitly programmed for each case.
Neural networks are computing systems loosely inspired by the brain. They consist of layers of nodes that process and transform data.
Earth is the third planet from the Sun. It has one moon, liquid water, and is the only known planet with life. Diameter: ~12,742 km.
The Moon is Earth's only natural satellite. It's about 384,000 km away. Humans last walked on it in 1972 (Apollo 17).
The Sun is a G-type star at the center of our solar system. It's about 150 million km from Earth. Its energy comes from nuclear fusion.
Our solar system has 8 planets: Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, Neptune. Plus dwarf planets like Pluto.
Mars is the fourth planet from the Sun — the Red Planet. It has the tallest volcano (Olympus Mons) and deepest canyon (Valles Marineris) in the solar system.
A black hole is a region of space where gravity is so strong nothing, not even light, can escape. They form from collapsed massive stars.
A galaxy is a system of billions of stars, gas, and dust held together by gravity. Our galaxy is the Milky Way.
The Milky Way is our home galaxy. It contains 100-400 billion stars. The solar system is about 26,000 light-years from the galactic center.
Gravity is the force that attracts objects with mass toward each other. Einstein described it as the curvature of spacetime.
The speed of light in a vacuum is 299,792,458 m/s (about 300,000 km/s). Nothing with mass can reach or exceed this speed.
Atoms are the basic units of matter. They consist of protons and neutrons in the nucleus, with electrons orbiting around it.
DNA (deoxyribonucleic acid) is the molecule that carries genetic instructions. It has a double helix structure made of base pairs: A-T and C-G.
Evolution is the process of species changing over generations through natural selection. Proposed by Charles Darwin in 1859.
Photosynthesis is how plants convert sunlight, water, and CO2 into glucose and oxygen. It happens in chloroplasts using chlorophyll.
Water (H2O) is two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom. It covers ~71% of Earth's surface. Essential for all known life.
Oxygen (O2) makes up about 21% of Earth's atmosphere. It's essential for respiration in animals and combustion.
CO2 is a greenhouse gas produced by respiration and burning fossil fuels. Plants absorb it in photosynthesis.
Climate change refers to long-term shifts in global temperatures and weather patterns, largely driven by human greenhouse gas emissions since the industrial revolution.
World War II (1939-1945) was the deadliest conflict in history. It involved most of the world's nations, ended with Allied victory, and resulted in ~70-85 million deaths.
World War I (1914-1918) was triggered by the assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand. It introduced trench warfare and killed ~20 million people.
The American Revolution (1775-1783) was the war in which the 13 American colonies gained independence from Britain. The Declaration of Independence was signed in 1776.
The French Revolution (1789-1799) overthrew the monarchy and established a republic. It led to the rise of Napoleon Bonaparte.
The Cold War (1947-1991) was a geopolitical tension between the US and Soviet Union. It involved the space race, arms race, and proxy wars, but no direct military conflict between the superpowers.
NASA's Apollo 11 mission landed the first humans on the Moon on July 20, 1969. Neil Armstrong and Buzz Aldrin walked on the surface; Michael Collins orbited above.
The Roman Empire was one of history's largest empires, dominating Europe, North Africa, and the Middle East from 27 BC to 476 AD (Western Empire). Latin was its language.
Ancient Egypt was one of the world's earliest civilizations. The Great Pyramid of Giza, built ~2560 BC, is the only surviving wonder of the ancient world.
Dinosaurs dominated Earth from ~230 to 66 million years ago. They went extinct after a massive asteroid impact. Birds are actually modern dinosaurs.
Abraham Lincoln was the 16th US President (1861-1865). He led the Union through the Civil War and issued the Emancipation Proclamation, abolishing slavery.
Albert Einstein was a theoretical physicist who developed the theory of relativity (E=mc²). He won the 1921 Nobel Prize in Physics.
Isaac Newton discovered gravity (inspired by an apple), developed calculus, and formulated the three laws of motion. He lived 1643-1727.
Africa is the world's second-largest continent with 54 countries. It has the Sahara Desert, Nile River, and is often called the cradle of humanity.
Europe is a continent of 44 countries. It's home to the EU, NATO, and some of the world's oldest civilizations and cultures.
Asia is the world's largest continent by area and population. Home to China, India, Japan, and more. Contains the Himalayas and the world's tallest mountain, Everest.
Mount Everest is Earth's highest mountain at 8,848.86 meters (29,031.7 ft) above sea level. It's in the Himalayas on the Nepal-Tibet border.
The Amazon River in South America is the world's largest river by volume. The Amazon Rainforest produces ~20% of the world's oxygen.
The Sahara is the world's largest hot desert, covering most of North Africa — about the size of the United States.
Washington, D.C. is the capital of the United States. New York City is the largest city, but it's not the capital.
Paris is the capital of France. It's home to the Eiffel Tower, the Louvre, and Notre-Dame Cathedral.
Tokyo is the capital of Japan. It's the world's most populous metropolitan area with over 37 million people.
The Fibonacci sequence: 0, 1, 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, 21... Each number is the sum of the two before it. It appears in nature (flowers, shells, spirals).
Pi (π) is the ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter. It's approximately 3.14159... and goes on infinitely without repeating.
The Pythagorean theorem: a² + b² = c². In a right triangle, the square of the hypotenuse equals the sum of squares of the other two sides.
A prime number is divisible only by 1 and itself. Examples: 2, 3, 5, 7, 11, 13. There are infinitely many primes.
Binary is base-2, using only 0s and 1s. Computers use it because transistors have two states: on (1) and off (0). 1010 in binary = 10 in decimal.
Hexadecimal is base-16 (0-9, A-F). Used in CSS colors (#FF5733), memory addresses, and encoding. FF in hex = 255 in decimal.
A photon is a particle of light. It has no mass and always travels at the speed of light. It behaves as both a wave and a particle.
Quantum mechanics describes how matter and energy behave at the subatomic level. Things exist in superposition (multiple states) until observed.
Einstein's relativity: Special relativity says the speed of light is constant for all observers (E=mc²). General relativity says gravity curves spacetime.
The periodic table organizes all known chemical elements by atomic number. There are 118 confirmed elements. Hydrogen (H) is the lightest; Oganesson (Og) is the heaviest.
Bitcoin is a decentralized digital currency created in 2009 by the pseudonymous Satoshi Nakamoto. It uses blockchain — a public ledger of all transactions.
A blockchain is a distributed ledger where data is stored in linked blocks. It's tamper-resistant because changing one block would break the chain.
NFTs (Non-Fungible Tokens) are blockchain-based certificates of ownership for digital assets. Their popularity peaked around 2021-2022.
A chatbot is software that simulates conversation. Rule-based chatbots (like me!) follow scripts. AI chatbots like Claude use language models.
An algorithm is a step-by-step procedure to solve a problem. Examples: sorting algorithms (quicksort, mergesort), search algorithms (binary search).
Big O notation describes algorithm efficiency. O(1) is constant time, O(n) is linear, O(n²) is quadratic, O(log n) is logarithmic (very good).
Recursion is when a function calls itself. It needs a base case to stop. Example: factorial(n) = n * factorial(n-1), base case: factorial(0) = 1.
Debugging is finding and fixing bugs. Tools: browser DevTools, console.log(), breakpoints, error stack traces. Common bugs: typos, off-by-one errors, null/undefined.
A memory leak happens when a program allocates memory but never frees it, causing memory usage to grow until the app crashes or slows down.
The CPU (Central Processing Unit) is the brain of a computer. It executes instructions. Speed is measured in GHz. More cores means better multitasking.
RAM (Random Access Memory) is fast, temporary storage your computer uses while running programs. More RAM = more apps open at once without slowdown.
SSDs (Solid State Drives) are fast storage that use flash memory. HDDs (Hard Disk Drives) use spinning platters and are slower but cheaper per GB.
Wi-Fi is wireless networking. 2.4GHz has longer range but slower speeds. 5GHz is faster but shorter range. Wi-Fi 6 is the latest widely-used standard.
Bluetooth is a short-range wireless technology for connecting devices (headphones, keyboards, mice). Range: ~10 meters. Bluetooth 5 is the current standard.
Chocolate comes from cacao beans. Dark chocolate has the most cacao. Milk chocolate adds milk powder and sugar. White chocolate has no cocoa solids.
Coffee is brewed from roasted coffee beans. It contains caffeine, which blocks adenosine receptors making you feel alert. Espresso is concentrated coffee.
Bread is made from flour, water, yeast, and salt. Yeast ferments sugars, producing CO2 bubbles that make the bread rise.
Pizza originated in Naples, Italy. It's made with dough, tomato sauce, and cheese. The Margherita pizza (tomato, mozzarella, basil) is the classic.
Sushi is Japanese dish combining vinegared rice with seafood, vegetables, or other toppings. Sashimi is raw fish without rice.
Dogs (Canis lupus familiaris) are domesticated wolves. There are 340+ recognized breeds. They've lived alongside humans for ~15,000 years.
Cats (Felis catus) are semi-domesticated and have been living with humans for ~10,000 years. They sleep 12-16 hours a day and have excellent night vision.
Monkeys and apes are primates. Apes (chimps, gorillas, orangutans, gibbons, humans) have no tails. Chimps share ~98.7% of DNA with humans. 🐒
Elephants are the world's largest land animals. They have remarkable memory, complex social structures, and use tools. They mourn their dead.
Whales are the largest animals. The blue whale is the largest ever, reaching 30m long. They're mammals that breathe air and are highly intelligent.
Sharks have existed for ~450 million years — older than trees! They have multiple rows of teeth and electroreceptors to sense prey.
Octopuses are highly intelligent mollusks with 8 arms, three hearts, blue blood, and the ability to change color and texture in milliseconds.
Music is organized sound and silence. It has rhythm, melody, and harmony. It's been part of human culture for at least 40,000 years.
The Beatles were a British rock band (John Lennon, Paul McCartney, George Harrison, Ringo Starr) that became the best-selling music artists of all time (1960-1970).
Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart (1756-1791) was a classical composer who wrote his first symphony at age 8. He composed over 600 works including operas, symphonies, and concertos.
Sports are competitive physical activities. The most popular globally: soccer/football, cricket, basketball, tennis, and the Olympics.
Soccer (football outside the US) is the world's most popular sport with ~3.5 billion fans. FIFA World Cup is its biggest tournament.
Basketball was invented by James Naismith in 1891. The NBA is the top professional league. Michael Jordan and LeBron James are the most celebrated players.
The modern Olympics started in 1896 in Athens. The Summer and Winter Olympics each happen every 4 years (staggered by 2 years). Paris hosted in 2024.
Meditation is a practice of focused attention and mindfulness. Research shows it reduces stress, improves focus, and changes brain structure over time.
Adults need 7-9 hours of sleep. Sleep is when the brain consolidates memories, clears waste products, and repairs the body. Lack of sleep impairs everything.
Regular exercise improves cardiovascular health, mental health, sleep, and longevity. Even 30 minutes of walking per day has significant health benefits.
Vitamins are essential micronutrients. Vitamin C boosts immunity, D is needed for bone health (often deficient), B12 is crucial for nerve function, A for vision.
That's me! 🐒 Monkey Joe is a rules-based AI assistant built by Akiva with help from Claude. My brain grows as Akiva adds more to it!
